Moran family.description: Australian criminal family based in Melbourne, founded by Lewis Moran in the 1970s. Involved in drug trafficking, corruption, money laundering, and debt collection. Notable for their role in the Melbourne gangland killings, which resulted in over 30 deaths from 1998 to 2010. The family has suffered multiple losses, including patriarch Lewis Moran and his sons Jason and Mark.

descriptions: The Carlton Crew was allied with the Moran family.

East Harlem Purple Gang.description: The East Harlem Purple Gang was an Italian-American organized crime group based in New York City, active from the early 1970s to the 1980s. It specialized in heroin trafficking, murder, and contract killing, dominating drug distribution in East Harlem and the Bronx. Though semi-independent, it had close ties with the Genovese crime family and was considered an unofficial sixth family. The gang had about 30 members and 80 associates at its peak.

descriptions: The 116th Street Crew is allied with the Purple Gang (East Harlem Purple Gang).
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Bonanno , Colombo , Gambino , Lucchese , Purple Gang

descriptions: The 116th Street Crew absorbed former members of the East Harlem Purple Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: By the late 1970s and early 1980s, the 116th Street Crew had absorbed and initiated many former members of the vicious East Harlem Purple Gang

descriptions: Remnants of the Purple Gang became part of the Genovese family's 116th Street Crew.
edges.evidence_quote: its remnants or former members became part of the Genovese family's 116th Street Crew

descriptions: There was a feared mob war between the Purple Gang and the Bonanno crime family under Carmine Galante.
edges.evidence_quote: law enforcement at one point feared that a mob war would break out between the Purple Gang and certain Mafia families, specifically Carmine Galante's Bonanno Crime Family

descriptions: The Purple Gang later worked with the Bonanno crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: later with the Bonanno crime family

descriptions: The Purple Gang acted as major drug distributors for the Five Families.
edges.evidence_quote: major drug distributors for New York's 'Five Families'

edges.evidence_quote: The East Harlem Purple Gang ... was semi-independent of but closely connected to (and at times feuded with) the Italian-American Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: It developed its 'closest ties' with the Genovese family

Ping On.description: Boston-based Chinese criminal organization founded in the 1970s by Stephen 'Sky Dragon' Tse, who had ties to the 14K Triad. It engaged in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, fraud, prostitution, human trafficking, illegal gambling, money laundering, extortion, murder, illegal immigration, and racketeering. The organization declined after Tse's imprisonment in the 1980s and was linked to the Boston Chinatown massacre. It was active from the 1970s to the early 2000s.

descriptions: 14K has connections to Ping On leadership.
edges.evidence_quote: The 14K has had connections to the leadership of the Ping On triad in Boston

descriptions: Ping On served as a recruitment and training ground for 14K Triad.
edges.evidence_quote: The Ping On was regarded as a 'farm team' for the 14K triad in Hong Kong.

descriptions: Ping On leader Tse was allied with the head of the local Chee Kung Tong.
edges.evidence_quote: Tse was allied with Harry Mook (Goon Chun Yee), head of the local CKT

descriptions: Tse was a member of Hung Mun before founding Ping On, suggesting a predecessor relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: he joined the Hung Mun (Chee Kung Tong or CKT) social club in 1977, then founded the Ping On Club in 1982

descriptions: Ping On and Hung Mong were allies in the late 1980s.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization had several allies in the late 1980s, including the Hung Mong which was in operation in New York City.

descriptions: Tse briefly joined On Leong but was expelled, indicating a conflict or falling out.
edges.evidence_quote: Tse joined the On Leong, but they expelled him shortly afterward

Clan Fabbrocino.description: Mafia-type criminal organization originating from the Vesuvian area of Italy, between Ottaviano, San Gennaro Vesuviano and San Giuseppe Vesuviano. Founded by Mario Fabbrocino in the late 1970s, it controls a vast territory and engages in diversified illegal activities including money laundering, extortion, and vote-buying. The clan has been involved in violent conflicts with rival organizations such as the Nuova Camorra Organizzata.

descriptions: Fabbrocino clan is a Camorra clan.
edges.evidence_quote: the Fabbrocino clan and the Ascione clan

descriptions: Fabbrocino clan is a Camorra clan, part of the Camorra organization.
edges.evidence_quote: On 23 April 2019, the powerful boss Mario Fabbrocino , leader of the Fabbrocino clan, died in the hospital of the Parma prison

descriptions: Clan Fabbrocino is a Camorra clan involved in an alliance with Chinese gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: Tra i clan coinvolti in questa alleanza c'erano il clan dei Casalesi, il clan Fabbrocino e il clan Ascione.

descriptions: The two clans jointly carried out a revenge killing.
edges.evidence_quote: Per vendicarsi i Fabbrocino e gli Ascione avrebbero ucciso Salvatore Batti

descriptions: Clan Cava has alliances with Clan Russo and Clan Fabbrocino in the Nola area.
edges.evidence_quote: Ancora oggi, quello schieramento di parte si rispecchia nelle alleanze che i Cava hanno con altri gruppi criminali dell' agro nolano come i clan Russo di Nola e Fabbrocino

descriptions: The 'Ndrangheta supported Fabbrocino in the murder of Roberto Cutolo.
edges.evidence_quote: eliminando, a Tradate , con il supporto della 'ndrangheta calabrese ... Roberto Cutolo

descriptions: The Fabbrocino clan is a part of the Camorra.

edges.evidence_quote: The feud with Cutolo intensified when Cutolo ordered the killing of Fabbrocino's brother Francesco. Fabbrocino later avenged his brother's death by ordering the murder of Cutolo's only son, Roberto, on 19 December 1990.

descriptions: Chinese gangs allied with Fabbrocino clan in industrial waste export scheme.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2017, investigators uncovered a plan between the Camorra and Chinese gangs: these exported industrial waste from Italy to China which guaranteed revenues of millions of euros for both organizations. Industrial waste left Prato and arrived in Hong Kong. Among the clans involved in this alliance were the Casalesi clan, the Fabbrocino clan and the Ascione clan.

Aryan Brotherhood.description: The Aryan Brotherhood is a neo-Nazi prison gang and organized crime syndicate based in the United States, founded in 1964 at San Quentin State Prison. It has an estimated 15,000–20,000 members both inside and outside prisons, engaging in drug trafficking, extortion, murder-for-hire, and other criminal activities. The gang is whites-only and operates under a hierarchical structure with a twelve-man council and a three-man commission. It is considered one of the oldest and most notorious racist prison gangs in the U.S.

descriptions: Black P. Stones is a rival gang of Aryan Brotherhood.
edges.evidence_quote: Other rival gangs include the Black P. Stones, Bloods, Crips, D.C. Blacks, and Nuestra Familia.

edges.evidence_quote: The San Quentin State Aryan Brotherhood uses the gang Family Affiliated Irish Mafia (FAIM) for its street-operated activity.

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood established ties with Gambino crime family via John Gotti for protection and business partnership.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1992, the gang established ties with American Mafia crime, via boss John Gotti, who was sentenced to prison and contacted the Aryan Brotherhood for protection while he was in prison. Gotti also organized a business partnership which operated on the outside between his group and the Brotherhood and as a result of this business partnership, the group's power greatly expanded on the streets.

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood and Hells Angels were involved in a power struggle resulting in a killing.
edges.evidence_quote: The Brotherhood has associated in criminal ventures with the Hells Angels. The gangs were also involved in a power struggle in the East Bay, which led to the killing of Hells Angels vice-president Michael O'Farrell on June 6, 1989.

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood had a brief connection with Manson Family, attempted to join forces, but ended due to offense over Sharon Tate murder.
edges.evidence_quote: In the early 1970s, the Aryan Brotherhood had a connection with Charles Manson and the Manson Family. Several members of the Manson Family were in prison at the time, and they attempted to join forces. However, the relationship did not last long as the Aryan Brotherhood took offense at the murder of pregnant actress Sharon Tate.

descriptions: The text explicitly states the two groups are not affiliated.
edges.evidence_quote: Despite the similarity in their names, the Aryan Brotherhood of Texas is not affiliated with the Aryan Brotherhood (AB), a separate and also notorious prison gang and crime syndicate founded in California in the 1960s with branches in the federal and state prison systems and outside prison walls.

descriptions: ABT is not related to AB; AB denied ABT's request to start a chapter in Texas.
edges.evidence_quote: A pesar de la semejanza en sus nombres, la Hermandad Aria de Texas no está relacionada con el grupo homónimo (AB por sus siglas en inglés), una notoria pandilla de prisión y sindicato del crimen fundado en California en los años 60, con ramas en prisiones federales y estatales, así como fuera de prisión.

descriptions: Bandidos member's drug ring had links to Aryan Brotherhood.
edges.evidence_quote: Retired from the club, he again began dealing methamphetamine in 2007 and headed an organization with links to the Aryan Brotherhood and La Familia Michoacana which operated from Fort Worth until August 20, 2009, when nineteen members were indicted and arrested by the DEA.

descriptions: An Aryan Brotherhood affiliate influenced SAC's founder to start the gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Tracy had been influenced to establish a white power group after an Aryan Brotherhood affiliate introduced him to white supremacist literature sometime in the mid-to-late 1990s.

descriptions: Sureños are allied with Aryan Brotherhood.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: 18th Street gang, Armenian Power, Aryan Brotherhood, Avenues, El Monte Flores, Familia 27, Florencia 13, Forming Kaos, Gulf Cartel, Mexican Mafia, Mongols, Ontario Varrio Sur, Playboys, Puente 13, Santa Monica 13, Toonerville Rifa 13, Vineland Boys, White Fence, Temple Street (gang)

descriptions: Sureños are allied with Hermandad Aria.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Hermandad Aria

descriptions: Texas Syndicate formed in opposition to Aryan Brotherhood preying on Texas inmates.
edges.evidence_quote: The Texas Syndicate was established at Folsom State Prison in California in 1978 in direct response to the other California prison gangs, notably the Aryan Brotherhood and Mexican Mafia, which were attempting to prey on native Texas inmates.

descriptions: Texas Syndicate was formed in response to the Aryan Brotherhood, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: El Sindicato de Texas nació en la Prisión Estatal de Folsom en 1978 en el estado de California, como respuesta a otras pandillas que surgieron en la década, sobre todo a la Hermandad Aria y la Mafia Mexicana, que intentaban aprovecharse de los reclusos nativos de Texas.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
descriptions: The CSA had links to the Aryan Brotherhood.
edges.evidence_quote: The CSA had links to other radical organizations, including the Aryan Brotherhood, the Mountain Church, and The Order

descriptions: United Bamboo Triad is listed as an ally of Black Dragons in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Wah Ching , Vietnamese Boyz Gang, Oriental Lazy Boyz , Asian Boyz , Hop Sing Tong, United Bamboo Triad, 14k Triad
